critic.verdict: An outstanding and beautifully crafted Easy chart that perfectly balances physical comfort, rhythmic precision, and creative hold patterns.

issues.problem: The successive jump-holds (tapping one note while holding another) are highly engaging and physically comfortable, but they lean slightly on the upper difficulty limit for an Easy chart.

This step lands on an off-beat (half-beat offset). While it matches the vocal syncopation of the song's outro well, it may momentarily throw off novice players who are relying strictly on the downbeat.

critic.verdict: Accurately timed to the 110 BPM tempo, but severely held back by extremely lazy, repetitive circular patterns during the chorus.

issues.problem: Incredibly lazy and repetitive 4-note circular loop (D-U-L-R) repeated nearly 7 times consecutively, which feels highly mechanical and creates uncomfortable double-steps on pad.
Continues the lazy chorus design by simply reversing the circular pattern to D-U-R-L and repeating it, ignoring the expressive vocal melody.

critic.verdict: A rhythmically accurate and well-paced chart with great energy tracking, marred by highly awkward hold-shielded patterns that force impossible one-foot runs and brackets.

issues.problem: Holding R while tapping L then D on 16th notes forces the player to perform an extremely fast and uncomfortable one-foot transition (L to D) in under 140ms.
A JUMP DR is placed while holding U, resulting in an unexpected 3-panel hand or bracket requirement that feels out of place on a Hard difficulty chart.
While holding U, the chart features a 16th-note stream of L -> R -> D, forcing the player's single free foot to clumsily rush across three distinct panels in 270ms.
